goal: Port structural.types.sentinels (stringly_typed) to Structure; rename to canonical

summary.title: Port structural.types.sentinels to Structure; rename to canonical
summary.what: Adds Language.stringly_typed_params() override on Python/C/C++/Ruby grammars. Adds Structure.sentinel_parameters() view method + SentinelParameter frozen record + slop.structure._sentinels helper. Call-site enrichment is now substrate-native (AST walk over call_node_types + extract_callee_name), no external grep. Renames rule file stringly_typed.py → sentinels.py, function run_stringly_typed → run_sentinels, test test_stringly_typed.py → test_sentinels.py. Drops legacy_v2_shim. Deletes slop/_structural/stringly_typed.py (772 lines). Full suite 538/538.
summary.why: Seventh structural shim retirement plus naming-spec rename. The legacy kernel duplicated per-language parameter extraction across four files plus ran ripgrep externally for call-site enrichment. Substrate-native version: each grammar exposes one ``stringly_typed_params`` classmethod and call-site collection reuses Intent A's call_node_types vocabulary, eliminating both the per-language walker duplication and the external grep dependency.
summary.user_goal: Port structural.types.sentinels (stringly_typed) to Structure; rename to canonical

decisions.chose: Substrate-native call-site enrichment over external grep
decisions.rationale: Intent E (redundancy) added Language.call_node_types + extract_callee_name. Reusing those for call-site collection means the rule doesn't need ripgrep at all — every call is already in the parsed AST. Plus parser-aware accuracy: no false matches in comments or strings, and proper handling of method calls vs free calls (Ruby ``str.upcase`` vs ``upcase(str)``).

decisions.chose: Single per-grammar method ``stringly_typed_params`` over per-grammar override-by-vocabulary
decisions.rationale: Considered splitting into call_node_types + parameter_walker + string_type_predicate. Rejected: the parameter walking is grammar-specific in a way the substrate doesn't already expose (declarator chains for C/C++, typed_parameter shapes for Python, identifier-only for Ruby). One method per grammar keeps the per-language code together where it belongs.

decisions.chose: Ruby always returns annotated=False; substrate passes Ruby through regardless of require_str_annotation
decisions.rationale: Ruby is dynamically typed — there is no type annotation. With require_str_annotation=True, the universal filter would drop every Ruby candidate. The compute layer special-cases ``language == 'ruby'`` to keep Ruby sentinels visible. This matches the legacy kernel's behaviour, which never required annotation for Ruby.

decisions.chose: Token-aware ``str`` detection in Python (reuses _python_type_tokens from escape_hatches)
decisions.rationale: Pure equality (``type_text == 'str'``) would miss ``Optional[str]``, ``str | None``, ``Annotated[str, ...]``. The token-set check from Intent F's _python_type_tokens already exists and handles these cases. No duplication.

review_notes: The substrate-native call-site enrichment is more accurate than the legacy regex (which used ``\bfn_name\s*\([^)]*"..."`` and was line-bounded). The new walker handles multi-line calls and method-call shapes correctly.
_python_type_tokens (introduced in Intent F for escape_hatches) is reused for the ``str`` annotation token check — the helper sits at module level in python.py and works for both intents.

The 'Ruby always passes annotated=False' special case is documented in _sentinels.compute_sentinels and visible at the test layer (test_view_no_annotation_required_detects_untyped covers Python untyped; Ruby sentinels would always require require_str_annotation=False in a Ruby-only fixture).

architectural_claims: Per-grammar parameter walking is now a Language classmethod (stringly_typed_params); the substrate iterates callables once and aggregates.
Call-site enrichment is substrate-native — reuses the call_node_types + extract_callee_name vocabulary from Intent E (redundancy). No external ripgrep dependency on this rule path.
Ruby's dynamic-typing case is handled at the compute layer (annotated=False sentinels pass through require_str_annotation=True), preserving legacy semantics.
behavioral_changes: run_sentinels signature changes from (root, rc, sc) to (structure, rc, sc); function renamed from run_stringly_typed.
Rule file path changes from slop/structure/rules/stringly_typed.py to slop/structure/rules/sentinels.py.
Test file path changes from tests/test_rules/test_stringly_typed.py to tests/test_rules/test_sentinels.py.
Summary fields ``functions_analyzed`` and ``files_searched`` are removed; ``candidates_analyzed`` replaces them.
Call-site literal collection is now parser-aware (legacy was line-bounded regex); multi-line calls now resolve correctly.
migration_notes: Custom code importing run_stringly_typed from slop.structure.rules.stringly_typed will break. Replacement is slop.structure.rules.sentinels.run_sentinels.
Custom code calling stringly_typed_kernel directly will break (kernel deleted). Use Tree + Structure.sentinel_parameters() instead.
